title: Bus Driver

rationales.taskAutomation: Autonomous transit shuttles run on a handful of fixed, low-speed routes, but mixed-traffic city and highway bus routes with passenger safety obligations remain human-driven.
rationales.toolMaturity: Autonomous shuttle products (May Mobility and similar) exist and are improving, but remain low-speed, fixed-route pilots rather than general bus replacements.
rationales.adoption: A small number of transit agencies run autonomous shuttle pilots; the overwhelming majority of transit and intercity bus service is entirely human-driven.
rationales.agenticExposure: Scheduling and route-timing optimization are agent-assistable; the driving and passenger-safety core is not.

tasksAtRisk: Standard fixed-route driving on low-speed campus/shuttle lines
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 53-3052, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Autonomous shuttle pilots are real but confined to fixed, low-speed routes; mixed-traffic transit and intercity bus driving, with the safety and liability obligations of carrying passengers, is not close to automatable at production reliability.
